termination_reason: Lambda instance entered Alert state (Monitor/SSH became unreachable ~06:14 UTC); manually terminated afterward to stop billing.
key_finding: 7B + LoRA starts at saturation. Iter 1 mean_reward = 0.987 (already near-perfect, above the 0.968 rule-based baseline). Across all 5 completed iters, loss stays at 0 and KL stays at 0 because reward variance within each rollout group is near-zero. GRPO's group-relative advantage normalization produces zero advantage when rollouts are indistinguishable, so no gradient signal propagates to the LoRA adapter. This is not a bug: it is GRPO's upper operating boundary on this benchmark.

interpretation.window: Qwen2.5-1.5B is BELOW the window (lacks capacity; mean_reward oscillates around 0.5 across 50 iter without net improvement). Qwen2.5-7B is ABOVE the window (saturates at init). The useful GRPO training range for this benchmark likely sits around 3B-4B parameters — sufficient capacity to exceed task threshold, insufficient to immediately saturate.
interpretation.implication_for_agent_builders: If you want to use GRPO on ComtradeBench, pick a base model in the 3B-4B range. Smaller wastes compute; larger produces no gradient signal.
interpretation.implication_for_benchmark_designers: ComtradeBench currently has a 'frontier saturation ceiling' (Kimi = Claude at 97.5) that coincides with the GRPO training ceiling (7B saturates). Raising the ceiling — with harder T9 variants or T11+ tasks — would simultaneously restore cross-model discrimination and reopen GRPO's training window.

reference_runs: grpo_gradient_training.jsonl — Qwen2.5-1.5B, 50 iter, full-parameter training (below the window)
grpo_smoke_lora/metrics.jsonl — Qwen2.5-0.5B smoke test of the LoRA+disable_adapter code path
